title: Persist pooled_effective_N to pooled-run artifacts
why: Today's runtime-only stdout print means skeptical reviewers cannot reproduce effective-N without rerunning (verified 2026-07-03: re-derivation matched to ~4.5% only). The pooled effective-N vs the 799 floor is the decisive number in every pooled verdict this round (T002D1, dense-state baselines, ungated sparse cusum) and currently exists nowhere on disk.
value_rationale: Cheap (S) fix that protects reproducibility of the load-bearing pooled effective-N number used across every pooled audit and readout.
scope: Write raw_N / effective_N / refusal_floor per pool into a JSON at the pooled output root (additive; likely a small function in scripts/run_pooled_equity_d1.py after member build, NOT inside run_multi_h4.py)
Surface the same numbers in the pooled audit record

history.reason: logged from 2026-07-03 pooled-audit B0006 readout verification pass
